top10.introHtml: Ranked for the cheap fun second car lens: working top first, reliable model family second, miles flexible. All priced $7k–$15k within 35 miles of Media, PA as of 2026-06-15.

picks.whyGood: Only 11,656 miles on a 2003 Z4 is essentially extraordinary—this car has barely been driven. The Z4 2.5i is a proper rear-wheel-drive roadster with a smooth inline-six and excellent chassis. Fred Beans is a reputable multi-franchise dealer. At $12,875 for 11k miles, the per-mile price is absurd in the best possible way.
picks.whatToVerify: Age-related rubber degradation despite low mileage: check all seals, hoses, and soft-top operation carefully. Inspect tires (may be 10+ years old regardless of tread). Soft-top hydraulic system (common Z4 issue). Look for dry rot on suspension bushings. Confirm title matches VIN and odometer reading is legitimate.
picks.bottomLine: A near-mint-mileage Z4 roadster at a fair price—hardest part is believing the odometer, so get a CarFax and pre-purchase inspection.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.8 has the better M52 six-cylinder engine (vs the 2.3's weaker M52) and 75k miles is reasonable for a 25-year-old car. At under $8k it's arguably the best fun-per-dollar on this entire list. Only 15 miles away and no-accidents reported.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top condition and motor (E36-era tops wear out). Check for rust under the trunk floor and rocker panels—Z3s are susceptible. Cooling system (water pump, thermostat). Verify it has the 2.8 not the rarer M roadster engine. Ask for service history; timing chain guides on M52 can rattle when cold.
picks.bottomLine: Best value on the list: a real six-cylinder roadster for under $8k with low-enough mileage that it should have years left.

picks.whyGood: 45k miles on a 2004 Audi A4 Cabriolet is exceptional—these are relatively rare low-mileage examples. The B6 A4 cabriolet has a solid structure, nice interior, and the 1.8T is reliable when maintained. Clean title, no accidents, under $10k.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing belt service history (critical on 1.8T—replace if unknown). Soft-top condition: inspect rear window seam and all seals. Check for water intrusion in trunk under spare tire. Sludge risk on 1.8T if oil changes were skipped—pull dipstick and check oil quality. German electrics: windows, convertible top mechanism.
picks.bottomLine: Half the miles of most A4 Cabriolets at this price—worth the 28-mile drive to inspect it.

picks.whyGood: The MINI Cooper S gets the supercharged (R52 era) or turbocharged engine with real pep—much more fun than the base Cooper. 65k miles is genuinely low for a 2007. No accidents, clean title, under $8,500. Josie's Auto Sales in Gilbertsville carries interesting older cars.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through multiple full cycles (R52 tops have motor and latch issues). Check headliner for water staining. Inspect rear window seam. Power steering pump whine common on R52. Supercharger belt and tensioner if pre-2007 (some carry over). Coolant expansion tank cracking is a common failure—inspect closely.
picks.bottomLine: A MINI S with genuinely low miles at a compelling price—fun factor is high and parts availability is good.

picks.whyGood: Rated Great Deal by Cars.com pricing algorithm, clean title, no accidents, and Selden Motors is a known Willow Grove dealer. 75k miles on a Z3 is the sweet spot—broken in but not worn out. The 2.3 is adequate for back-road fun even if it's not the 2.8.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Z3 checklist as #2: soft-top motor and frame, trunk rust, cooling system. Check for oil leaks from valve cover gasket and rear main seal (very common on Z3). Verify power window operation (a common Z3 weak point).
picks.bottomLine: A well-priced, well-rated Z3 near Willow Grove—solid second-car choice if you don't need the extra power of the 2.8.

picks.whyGood: 44,000 miles on a 1994 GT is a genuine time-capsule find. SN-95 Mustangs are becoming collectible, parts are plentiful, and the 5.0 V8 GT is the one to have. Private seller often means better care history. At $10,500 this is priced fairly for the mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible-specific: check convertible top mounting brackets at the rocker panel junction (rot point). Inspect frame rails and floor for rust. Ask for original title/documentation. Test top through full cycle—top mechanism and latches wear out. Check rear seating area carpet for water staining.
picks.bottomLine: A legitimately low-mile SN-95 GT—if the top and structure are clean, this is a future classic at a fair price.

picks.whyGood: A Porsche Boxster for $11k only 11 miles away is hard to ignore. The 2001 is a 986 with the revised engine; 74k miles is reasonable. West Chester is close and private seller means you can ask detailed questions.
picks.whatToVerify: IMS bearing is the critical Boxster risk on 986s—ask if it has been replaced or if an IMS Solution/Guard bearing retrofit was done. Check for RMS (rear main seal) leakage. Inspect soft-top closely: rear plastic window, seams. Pull dipstick for coolant contamination (pre-check for bore scoring). Get a PPI from a Porsche specialist.
picks.bottomLine: Excellent Boxster price at very close range—worth the PPI cost; if IMS is addressed, this is the most car for the money on the list.

picks.whyGood: The newest MINI convertible on the list (F57 generation), just 8 miles away, with only 52k miles. Modern safety features, better electronics than R52 era, and MINI parts are easy to find. Private seller with reasonable price.
picks.whatToVerify: F57 MINI: operate top through full cycle including folding rear roof section. Check for oil leaks from valve cover (B38 engine common issue). Inspect all four corners for parking damage. Verify no active recalls (check NHTSA site with VIN). Ask for maintenance records—oil change interval is critical on these.
picks.bottomLine: The most modern, lowest-mileage daily-driveable pick on the list, and it's practically local.

picks.whyGood: A 2014 BMW 428i convertible for $10,555 with CarGurus Good Deal rating is significantly below market for this model. xDrive AWD adds all-season usability. The F33 body is a genuine grand tourer with a proper back seat.
picks.whatToVerify: Check for timing chain rattle on cold start (N20 engine known issue—critical). Inspect soft-top for tears and verify hydraulic pump operation. Check for coolant leaks at thermostat housing. Confirm all electronics work (iDrive, park assist, climate). Ask for service history—oil changes on N20 must be timely.
picks.bottomLine: Most car per dollar in the modern category—if the N20 timing chain is sound, this is a tremendous buy.

picks.whyGood: The E46 M3 is one of the greatest driving machines BMW ever built. A convertible M3 in the $7k-$15k range is almost impossible to find—143k miles on the S54 engine is high but within reason if maintained properly. This is the enthusiast pick: raw, rewarding, and appreciating.
picks.whatToVerify: S54 VANOS and SMG fluid if equipped (prefer 6-speed manual). Rod bearing inspection or replacement is non-negotiable at this mileage—budget $500-800 for the job. Throttle actuator (Throttle Valve Actuator) replacement is common. Subframe reinforcement—check if done. Soft-top mechanism and rear window. Get a PPI from an independent BMW specialist.
picks.bottomLine: Highest risk on the list but also highest reward—a properly maintained E46 M3 convertible is a bargain at any price under $15k.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in budget within 35 miles. Ranked GT > V6, lower miles preferred. All SN-95 and Fox-body GTs carry the real 5.0 V8.

picks.whyGood: 44k miles on a SN-95 GT is the kind of find people tell stories about. The 5.0 V8 sounds right, pulls well, and parts are everywhere. Private seller at a fair price.
picks.whatToVerify: Top frame rust at convertible mount-to-rocker junction (classic SN-95 failure point). Full top cycle test. Rear seat water staining. Check brake lines and fuel lines for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Best Mustang on the list by a wide margin—low miles, right engine, right trim.

picks.whyGood: Another SN-95 GT with relatively low mileage and a Philadelphia dealer at a fair price. The 5.0 GT was the last year of the pushrod V8 before the '96 4.6 swap—many purists prefer this engine.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checklist: convertible mount rust, top operation, rear drain channels. Ask GT Motors for maintenance records. Check T-5 or T-45 transmission for smooth gear engagement.
picks.bottomLine: Solid runner-up—nearly identical to #1 but dealer-sourced, adding some accountability.

picks.whyGood: A Fox-body GT convertible for under $10k with under 100k miles and a Great Deal rating. The 5.0 Fox is the quintessential cheap fun car—supremely hackable, tuneable, and supported. Close at 16 miles.
picks.whatToVerify: Fox bodies rust: check the floors, frame rails, and convertible bracing. Top condition and rear window. Brake master cylinder and proportioning valve. T-5 third-gear synchro (classic Fox weak point—slow-shift to 3rd to test).
picks.bottomLine: The cheapest V8 fun on the list—expect some imperfections but also expect smiles.

picks.whyGood: The S197 V6 makes 305 hp—more than the old GT—and gets good mileage. Only 9 miles away in West Chester. Under $12k with reasonable miles, and S197 Mustangs are proven reliable.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible: check rear seat water staining from top seal failure at back corners. IRS clunk on takeoff if so equipped. T-6060 or automatic—verify transmission operation. Check for any subframe cracks at convertible mount points.
picks.bottomLine: The most practical daily-driver Mustang pick—modern, close, and surprisingly quick.

picks.whyGood: Good Deal rating from CarGurus on a Premium trim with lower mileage than the #4 pick. Burns Honda is a franchised dealer, adding some peace of mind. The Premium gets better seats, audio, and appearance package.
picks.whatToVerify: Same S197 convertible checklist. Ask Burns Honda for Carfax and any trade-in inspection notes. Verify top seal condition at rear corners—most common water leak point on S197 convertibles.
picks.bottomLine: The freshest, best-equipped V6 Mustang on the list at a fair price from a reputable dealer.
